document: Senior Managers and Certification Regime (United Kingdom - Row 23)

sections.heading: Senior Managers and Certification Regime
sections.title: Senior Managers and Certification Regime framework for accountability
sections.summary: The Senior Managers and Certification Regime (SM&CR) establishes a framework to enhance accountability and market integrity by requiring individuals to take personal responsibility for their actions and improving conduct across all organizational levels. It applies to banks, building societies, credit unions, PRA-designated investment firms, insurers, and solo-regulated firms with specific exemptions. The regime does not require structural changes to governance or new hires but focuses on clarifying roles and responsibilities within firms.

requirements.text: Senior Managers and Certification Regime aims to reduce harm to consumers and strengthen market integrity
Encourage staff to take personal responsibility for their actions
Make sure firms and staff clearly understand and can show who does what

sections.source_quote: Senior Managers and Certification Regime aims to reduce harm to consumers and strengthen market integrity by making individuals more accountable for their conduct and competence.

sections.heading: Background
sections.title: Historical background of Senior Managers and Certification Regime
sections.summary: The Senior Managers and Certification Regime was established in response to the 2008 banking crisis and conduct failures like LIBOR manipulation. It was introduced by Parliament in December 2013 to address banking sector standards, initially applying to banks and building societies in March 2016, and later expanded to cover all financial services firms by May 2016. The regime has been reviewed multiple times to assess its effectiveness and impact on organizational accountability.

requirements.text: Parliament set up the Parliamentary Commission for Banking Standards (PCBS) to recommend ways to improve standards in the banking sector
PCBS recommended a new accountability framework focused on senior management
Firms take more responsibility for employees being fit and proper
There be better standards of conduct at all levels in banking firms
Parliament passed legislation in December 2013, leading to the FCA and PRA applying the SM&CR to the banking sector from March 2016
Parliament made further changes to legislation in May 2016, requiring extension of the regime to all FSMA-authorised firms

sections.source_quote: In response to the 2008 banking crisis and significant conduct failings such as the manipulation of LIBOR, Parliament set up the Parliamentary Commission for Banking Standards (PCBS) to recommend ways to improve standards in the banking sector.

sections.title: Links to related Senior Managers and Certification Regime resources
sections.summary: This section provides links to additional resources related to the Senior Managers and Certification Regime, including fitness and propriety frameworks, categorization of solo-regulated firms, culture and governance guidance, and application procedures for senior management roles. These links support firms in understanding and implementing the regime's requirements.
